label: PASS
rationale: The trajectory sets ReadLockEnabled and WriteLockEnabled true, ReadLocked and WriteLocked false, and LockOnReset to {0}, then performs a successful PowerCycle. The cited reset-state rules require a matching reset from S3/T4 to recover to S1 with the corresponding locked value true and the same LockOnReset value, so the Get returning ReadLocked true, WriteLocked true, and LockOnReset {0} is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: After the range is configured with read/write locking enabled, both Locked values false, and LockOnReset {0}, the successful PowerCycle is a matching reset. The reset-state rules require recovery with the corresponding Locked value true and LockOnReset preserved, so a Get returning ReadLocked false and WriteLocked false is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The setup leaves the LBA range read-lock enabled with LockOnReset {0}; after a successful PowerCycle the matching reset should make ReadLocked true. The ReadLocked rule says the TPer shall not allow requests to read user data when the enabled read lock is true, so a successful read of LBA 80-87 is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The setup leaves the LBA range write-lock enabled with LockOnReset {0}; after a successful PowerCycle the matching reset should make WriteLocked true. The WriteLocked rule says the TPer shall not allow requests to write user data when the enabled write lock is true, so a successful write to LBA 80-87 is not compliant.
